title: Information Security & Data Residency Policy
status: stub — chapter content to be populated
description: Security and hosting-location policy behind contract positions on data residency, access control, customer security schedules, incident timelines and third-party/sub-outsourced services.

chapters.title: Scope & Security Governance
chapters.synopsis: Policy scope, ownership and review cycle; relationship to POL-PRIV-001 and the security commitments Northgate is permitted to make in contracts.

chapters.title: Data Residency & Hosting Locations
chapters.synopsis: Approved hosting regions and residency commitments: EEA-only or UK-only data-centre restrictions that may be offered (e.g. payroll data UK-only), and EU-hosting commitments used as a governing-law fallback concession.

chapters.title: Access Control & Least Privilege
chapters.synopsis: Limits on counterparty and supplier access to Northgate systems and data — e.g. support access confined to ticket data, production-database access refused; authentication and privileged-access standards.

chapters.title: Customer Security Schedules & Exceptions
chapters.synopsis: How to review a customer's security schedule against Northgate's control set, which requirements can be accepted, and how exceptions are documented and approved.

chapters.title: Incident Response & Notification Timelines
chapters.synopsis: Security-incident classification and response; standard contractual breach-notification window of 48 hours (12/24-hour demands to be negotiated up), aligned with POL-PRIV-001 chapter 8.

chapters.title: Supplier & Sub-Outsourcing Security
chapters.synopsis: Security requirements flowed down to suppliers and sub-outsourced services, including notification obligations for sub-outsourcing and requirements for regulated customers (FCA SYSC 8).

chapters.title: Security Testing & Authorised Access
chapters.synopsis: Rules for penetration testing and security assessments: prior written authorisation, tightly-defined scope boundaries (Computer Misuse Act exposure), and designation of findings as Confidential Information.

chapters.title: Data Classification & Handling
chapters.synopsis: Northgate's classification scheme (public / internal / confidential / restricted), handling and labelling rules per class, and how classification maps to the residency and access commitments in chapters 2–3.

chapters.title: Personnel Security & Training
chapters.synopsis: Pre-employment screening, confidentiality undertakings, security training at induction and annual refresh, and leaver/mover access revocation aligned with the access-control standards in chapter 3.

chapters.title: Business Continuity & Disaster Recovery
chapters.synopsis: Backup, recovery and continuity arrangements underpinning the business-continuity commitments offered in contracts (in place of source-code escrow), including RTO/RPO targets and annual test requirements.

chapters.title: Exceptions, Review & Document Control
chapters.synopsis: Risk-acceptance and waiver process for deviations from this policy (Security owner sign-off, time-limited), monitoring and internal audit, annual review cycle and version history.
